title: Korean Aerated Rice Mixed-Bowl
description: A tangy, aerated Korean dish featuring a mix of vegetables, tofu, and rice, served in a bamboo tray, perfect for a light and refreshing meal.

ingredients.name: Gochujang
ingredients.notes: Korean chili paste for added depth

instructions: Start by rinsing the rice and soaking it in cold water for at least 2 hours.
Drain and rinse the rice, then place it in a saucepan with 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and cover, simmering for 18-20 minutes, or until the water has been absorbed and the rice is cooked.
In a separate pan, heat 1 tablespoon of sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and sauté for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the diced red bell pepper, sliced cucumber, grated carrots, and cubed tofu, and stir-fry for 5-7 minutes, until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the gochujang, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, vegetable and tofu mixture, and toasted sesame seeds.
Garnish with thinly sliced green onions and serve immediately.

To assemble the bowl, place a scoop of the rice mixture in the center of the bamboo tray, followed by the vegetable and tofu mixture, and topped with additional garnishes such as pickled ginger or kimchi.
